title: Why Is Dycom Stock Skyrocketing Wednesday?
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Dycom Industries (DY) stock surged 27.95% to a new 52-week high after reporting strong Q1 earnings that beat expectations. Contract revenue rose 56.1% YoY to $1.97B, adjusted earnings jumped 84.9% to $4.42 per share, and the company raised its fiscal 2027 revenue guidance to $7.38-$7.65B. The growth was driven by robust demand for fiber infrastructure and data center construction. Dycom also announced a $275M acquisition of National Technology Integrators to expand its digital infrastructure capabilities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 earnings significantly exceeded analyst expectations with 56.1% YoY contract revenue growth and 84.9% earnings growth. Company raised full-year guidance above consensus estimates and announced a strategic acquisition. Stock reached new 52-week high with 27.95% single-day gain, reflecting investor confidence in the company's growth trajectory driven by robust demand for fiber and data center infrastructure.

title: Why Dycom Industries Stock Exploded Today
author: Rich Smith
description: Dycom Industries stock surged 27.12% after beating analyst forecasts with $4.42 EPS on $2 billion in sales, significantly exceeding Wall Street's expectations of $2.72 EPS on $1.7 billion. The company raised full-year guidance to $7.5 billion in revenue, driven by record demand for fiber infrastructure and data center builds in the AI era. With a 46.5% year-over-year increase in backlog to $11.9 billion, the company is positioned for continued strong growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company significantly beat analyst expectations on both earnings ($4.42 EPS vs. $2.72 forecast) and sales ($2B vs. $1.7B forecast), raised full-year guidance, reported 56% YoY sales growth with 46.5% YoY backlog increase, and CEO highlighted record demand for fiber and data center infrastructure driven by AI adoption.

title: Dycom Industries, Inc. Reports Record First Quarter Results and Raises Full Year Fiscal 2027 Outlook
author: Na
description: Dycom Industries reported record first quarter fiscal 2027 results with contract revenues of $1.965 billion (56.1% growth, 24.7% organic) and net income of $91.3 million. The company raised its full year fiscal 2027 outlook and announced the acquisition of National Technology Integrators for $275 million to expand capabilities in the high-growth data center industry. Strong demand for fiber infrastructure and data center builds drove performance across both segments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company delivered record Q1 results exceeding guidance with 56.1% revenue growth, 49.5% net income growth, record backlog of $11.9 billion (+46.5%), margin expansion, and raised full year outlook. Strategic acquisition of National Technology Integrators for $175M annual revenue run-rate further strengthens market position in high-growth data center sector.

title: Dycom Industries, Inc. to Host Fiscal 2027 First Quarter Results Call on May 27, 2026
author: Na
description: Dycom Industries (NYSE: DY) announced it will host a conference call on May 27, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. ET to discuss fiscal 2027 first quarter results. The company will issue a press release that morning, with participants able to join via registration or listen to a live webcast. A replay will be available for 120 days on the company's investor website.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article is a routine announcement of an earnings conference call with no forward-looking statements, guidance, or operational updates that would indicate positive or negative performance. It is purely procedural in nature, providing logistical details for investors to participate in the earnings discussion.

title: Dycom Caps Record Year With $9.5 Billion Backlog
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Dycom Industries reported strong fourth-quarter results with contract revenues up 34.4% YoY to $1.46 billion, beating estimates. The company ended the year with a record $9.5 billion backlog and announced the completion of its Power Solutions acquisition, positioning it in the data center market. For fiscal 2027, Dycom expects sales of $6.85-7.15 billion. Despite strong earnings, shares fell 4.35% following a 152% surge over the past year.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company delivered record results with 34.4% revenue growth, beat EPS estimates ($2.03 vs $1.73), achieved record $9.5B backlog, and provided FY2027 guidance above analyst expectations. Strategic Power Solutions acquisition expands into high-growth data center market. Strong operational metrics including 39.6% EBITDA growth and $419M operating cash flow demonstrate solid business momentum.
